Rhino is the best early attacker. Always click Mobile Animus.

Early Hannibul rush is unstoppable.

Cluster Bolt is good even when you have Polywall and the opponent has 5 attack.
You don't need Tarsiers in an Amporilla set.
Blood Pact is the best defense in the game.
It's good to spend limited Chill even if it denies 0 absorb.

Note also that what I suggested allows to choose 2 turns in which to gain an extra gold. This allows higher resolution for fine-tuning, but also increases the total possible. [2022-08-08 21:35] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): Maybe it can be abstracted. Instead of choosing specific timings, the bid is on a general measure from -1 to +1 of the advantage of P1/P2. And then the actual bonus will be somewhat random but correspond to this value.

For example, if the winning bid is 0.5, then maybe you will get 1 gold on turn 3, or maybe you get 1 gold on turn 2 and the opponent gets 1 gold on turn 5, or some other combination around this average value.

So you're not encouraged to think of specific builds when bidding, just the overall advantage of each player. Only when the bidding is over and the actual bonus is set, you can think of a build that utilizes it.
[2022-08-08 21:37] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): It might not be perfect, but it could be better than the current system where some sets clearly favor one player or the other.

^ Speaking from what I've heard, not from experience... My level is low enough that any advantage to one player is negligible compared to the impact of the mistakes I make.
[2022-08-08 21:37] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): But I do wish for a system that is more satisfactory for higher-level players.

[2022-08-29 14:59] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): Prismata is similar to (and inspired by, IIRC) Dominion in the "stocks"/set aspect, but that's pretty much where the similarity ends.

Prismata is not a deckbuilder or even close to it mechanics-wise. There is no deck, or indeed, as Awaclus mentioned, any sort of randomness or hidden information.

Once the set is determined, Prismata is a so-called "combinatorial game", like Chess or Go. As such, the challenges it poses are different than those of a deck game.

A tangible difference is that in Prismata, a much stronger player will almost always win against a weaker player (if no handicap is set). In Dominion, a weaker player will win some matches due to luck of the draw.
